description: seed bagging (3 seeds) + lightgbm + multi-model SHAP + calibration + class-weight search + 10pct HPO
notes: first validation run of the rewritten pipeline; public LB slightly below prior baseline (0.94960/0.94956)

v5: features from SHAP review + meta double-balance fix + weight-search guard; LB 0.94951 beats v4 (0.94915) ~= baseline (0.94960); categorical crosses were the win; meta class_weight=None backfired (guard fell back to simple_avg)

description: v6: meta reverted to balanced + LR-excluded blend candidates
notes: v6: fix meta class_weight, blend picks best of avg_all/avg_strong/meta/best_base; avg_strong (LR excluded) won OOF 0.94970 but LB 0.94944 < v5 0.94951 -- OOF gain did NOT transfer, LR diversity helps LB generalization; at noise floor ~0.9495

description: v7: 3-way crosses + cat-x-bin crosses + within-group deviations + numeric products
notes: v7: new feature families (3-way crosses, cat-x-bin, within-group deviations, numeric products). New feats rank HIGH in SHAP (stress_activity_sleepqual #2, stress_x_sleepcat #4, sleep_vs_stress #9) but base-learner OOF did NOT improve (final 0.94954 < v6 0.94970); the crosses just redistribute signal already captured (stress_x_activity fell #1->#5). LB 0.94934 < v5 0.94951 and v6 0.94944 -- confirms redundant, no lift; easy FE signal exhausted, at noise floor. Best remains v5 LB 0.94951

description: v8: missingness flags + exercise_zero + calorie-mode features; proba artifacts for blending
notes: v8: EDA-driven features (per-column missing flags + n_missing, exercise_zero, cal_low_mode x activity) from vad13irt EDA; 54 features survived SHAP. LB 0.94931 < v7 0.94934 < v5 0.94951 -- no lift, missingness looks MCAR after all; still at the ~0.9495 noise floor. LGB best base (0.94947); LR up to 0.92191 (missing flags helped only the linear learner). First run archiving test_proba/oof_proba for ply-s6e7-blend.ipynb (preds/fde706f0); LB submitted file-directly (kernel-version submit failed under --no-push)

description: v9: XGBoost + RealMLP stack (RealMLP port from public LB-0.95090 notebook)
notes: v9: dropped cat/lr/lgb/FT base learners, stack is XGB + from-scratch RealMLP port (no pytabkit; PBLD embeddings, NTPLinear n_ens=8, EMA, flat_cos). 54 features via SHAP consensus. Runtime 3038s vs v8 10568s (3.5x faster). blend_source avg_all; calibration hurt (0.94324) so off. LB 0.94964 -- NEW BEST (v5 0.94951). Kernel-version submit 400s (CreateCodeSubmission); submitted submission.csv file-directly. Second proba-artifact run (preds/b6f6f8b1) -> blender now has 2 runs

description: v10: v5 rerun with seed 777 (proba artifacts for blending)
notes: v10 = exact v5 pipeline (dfac21e), only outer-stack seed changed 42->777; RNG=42 kept for HPO/SHAP. Goal: diverse-seed sibling of best run (v5 LB 0.94951) for cross-submission blending. Kernel version 10

DEGENERATE BLEND: OOF hill-climb over v8+v9+v10 put weight 1.0 on fde706f0 (v8, best OOF 0.94959); blended predictions identical to the v8 submission (LB 0.94931) so NOT resubmitted. Simple-avg of the 3 runs: OOF 0.94947 < best single. OOF-guided blending finds no gain -- consistent with v6 lesson; note OOF ranking (v8 best) inverts LB ranking (v9 best, 0.94964). weights 5a170c55=0.0, b6f6f8b1=0.0, fde706f0=1.0

description: v11: FT-PLR (FT-Transformer + PLR embeddings; per-value TE of raw 13 cols) as 3rd base learner + per-learner proba artifacts
notes: E1 from experiment plan; GPU quota reset day

Library OOF (szymonkapiski, same 5-fold split rs=42) blended with our FT-PLR test proba. Fixed weights (few DOF -> transfers). NEW BEST LB 0.95085 (v11 0.95068). FT-PLR too correlated w/ their RealMLPs to add much; pub+fs alone=0.95075 OOF.

description: v13/E3: native-cat CatBoost + minority-vs-rest specialists as base learners (5-way stack); novel proba artifacts for blend/probe
v14: NODE (Neural Oblivious Decision Ensembles) novel architecture as base learner; lean trio xgb+ftplr+node
notes: novel-arch run; neural oblivious trees for blend decorrelation
single-model file submission; fill public_lb_score after submitting
